channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Scientists Detect Sugar in Milky Way, Shedding Light on Life's Origins
item.description: A team of astrochemists has identified naturally occurring sugar, specifically erythrulose, in the interstellar medium near the center of the Milky Way. This discovery, published in *Nature Astronomy*...

item.title: List of Hollywood Actors Known for Being Difficult to Work With
item.description: The article lists 15 Hollywood actors renowned for their challenging behavior on set. Chevy Chase is noted for his fiery confrontations, while Edward Norton's clashes with directors over creative cont...

item.title: Spanish PM highlights need for wildfire prevention strategies
item.description: The Spanish Prime Minister has called for enhanced prevention measures to mitigate the risk of deadly wildfires, following a recent incident that claimed lives. The statement emphasizes the importance...

item.title: PIC Suspends CEO and CIO Following Governance Crisis
item.description: The Public Investment Corporation (PIC), South Africa's largest asset manager overseeing R3.6 trillion in government pension and social funds, has placed its chief executive Patrick Dlamini and chief ...

item.title: Sen. Rick Scott Criticizes Senate Workweek and Recesses
item.description: Sen. Rick Scott (R-Florida) has publicly criticized the U.S. Senate for its short workweek and excessive recesses, arguing that the chamber's operations are inefficient and detrimental to legislative ...

item.title: Zanzibar's President Urges Tanzanian Firms to Expand Global Trade
item.description: Zanzibar’s President Dr Hussein Ali Mwinyi has called on Tanzanian businesses to expand their operations beyond domestic borders to tap into international trade opportunities. Speaking at the 50th Dar...

item.title: Freshfields Restructures Pay, Cuts Partnerships Amid Growth Efforts
item.description: UK law firm Freshfields has implemented significant changes to its partnership structure following a major overhaul of its compensation system. The restructuring, which introduced a performance-based ...

item.title: Trump Ends Press Conference After Confrontation with CNN Anchor Over Iran Strikes
item.description: President Donald Trump abruptly ended a White House press conference on Monday after CNN anchor Kaitlan Collins pressed him about U.S. military actions in Iran. During the Oval Office briefing, Collin...

item.title: U.S. Senator’s Sister Assumes Senate Seat After Legal Process
item.description: The article reports that Lindsey Graham’s sister has been selected to assume the remaining duties of his U.S. Senate seat following legal procedures. This decision comes after Graham's unexpected abse...

item.title: Xyriel Manabat Shares Personal Favorites and Fun Facts in S.O.S.H Slambook Feature
item.description: The article features actress Xyriel Manabat in a light and personality-driven edition of the S.O.S.H Slambook, where she shares some of her personal favourites, memorable experiences, and candid thoug...

item.title: Parents of raped 5-year-old demand justice as police ask for N50k to investigate case
item.description: Parents of 5-year-old Hauwa’u Hayatu, who was allegedly raped in Jos North, are trapped in a crisis after police demanded N50,000 to open an investigation. The victim’s mother, Suwaiba Iliyasu, reveal...

item.title: Reform UK criticizes Commons speaker over MPs’ security concerns following murder
item.description: Following the tragic murder of Ann Widdecombe, Reform UK has intensified its criticism of House of Commons Speaker Lindsay Hoyle, alleging that MPs are not receiving adequate security measures. The co...

item.title: Sam Neill's Iconic Jurassic Park Moment and Legacy
item.description: The article commemorates New Zealand actor Sam Neill, who passed away at 78 after battling lymphoma. It highlights his iconic performance in 'Jurassic Park,' particularly the unforgettable scene where...

item.title: Top STEM Schools in Kenya: Ranking and Highlights for 2026
item.description: The article discusses the ranking of STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics) schools in Kenya, emphasizing their role in fostering innovation and preparing students for future careers...

item.title: Municipal officials face personal liability for R1.7bn pension shortfall
item.description: The Motor Industry Staff Association (Misa) has demanded that municipal managers, mayors, and councillors be held personally accountable for a R1.7bn pension shortfall, accusing them of stealing from ...

item.title: Junior Boks stage remarkable comeback to reach U-20 World Championship final after beating England
item.description: The South African Junior Springboks delivered a stunning second-half performance to defeat England 53-37 and secure their place in the U-20 Rugby World Championship final against France. Trailing 20-1...

item.title: Police investigating circumstances surrounding British MP's death
item.description: Australian authorities are examining the circumstances leading to the death of a British Member of Parliament following reports that counter-terrorism units believe she was attacked prior to her passi...

item.title: JMFyang's Lyric Challenge Promotes Positive Vibes and Kilig
item.description: The article highlights JMFyang's participation in a lyric challenge that emphasizes positive energy and kilig (a Filipino term for romantic or sweet vibes). The challenge, part of ABS-CBN's entertainm...

item.title: Tinubu pays tribute to Buhari’s legacy of integrity and leadership
item.description: President Bola Tinubu paid heartfelt tribute to the late former President Muhammadu Buhari, highlighting his transformation from military ruler to civilian democrat as a rare example in Nigeria’s hist...

item.title: Report Says Apple Agreed to Use Intel's U.S. Manufacturing as Part of Tariff Negotiations with the White House
item.description: According to reports citing The Wall Street Journal, Apple agreed to have some of its custom chips manufactured at Intel's U.S. fabrication facilities after discussions with the White House during neg...
